mappings.rationaleAI: Visual perception of lightness, colour, form, depth, and motion addresses vision (B.2) and perceptual processes (B.2.c). Perceptual illusions address G and G.1. Contemporary theories address C.1 and C.2. Gestalt organization addresses C.3.a. Context effects address C.3.b. Perceptual constancy addresses C.3.c. Depth and motion perception address E and F.

Retinal anatomy addresses the work of the retina (B.2.a). Conversion to neural messages addresses the transduction process (B.2.b). Pattern recognition addresses perceptual processes (B.2.c). Optic nerve connections address cortical responses (B.2.d). Three-dimensional perception addresses depth perception (E.2).
Cerebral cortex and sensory processing address physiological mechanisms of attention (A.5) and the senses in general (B.1). Cortical responses address B.2.d.
Neuroscience of attention addresses A, A.1, A.2, A.3, and A.5. Brain imaging addresses sensation (B, B.1). Cognitive components connect to perception (C).
Neural correlates and attention mechanisms address A and A.1. Altered states address hallucinations (G.2), neurological factors (G.2.a), and types of hallucinatory experience (G.2.b).
Sound physics and hearing mechanisms address ear mechanisms (B.3.a). Pitch, loudness, and localization address auditory nerve processing (B.3.b).
Physics of light provides the foundation for understanding vision (B.2).
Neuroscience of pain perception addresses the sensory experience of pain (B.4.d) and cutaneous senses (B.4).
Olfaction physiology covering neural processing and genetic underpinnings directly addresses the olfactory sense (B.8).
Sensory perception addresses the senses in general (B.1) and sensation (B). Motor responses connect to kinesthesis (B.5).
Sensory organs address the senses in general (B.1). Ear anatomy connects to B.3.a. Vestibular structures address B.6.
Cross-modal sensory experiences address illusory experience (G.1) and perceptual illusions (G). Individual variations address differences in perceptual functioning (C.4).
Memory systems connect to factors affecting time perception (D.3) through temporal processing and subjective duration.
The relationship between thought and perception addresses attention (A) and perception processes (C).
Cognitive psychology covering perception, attention, and memory addresses A, B, C. Individual differences address C.4.
Electromagnetic radiation provides the basis for vision (B.2). Sound waves address audition (B.3).
Perceptual mechanisms of musicality address audition (B.3) and pitch/loudness processing (B.3.b). Aesthetic responses address perception (C).
Brain mechanisms generating dreams address neurological factors in hallucinations (G.2.a) and types of hallucinatory experience (G.2.b).
Sensory processing differences address sensation (B). Perceptual strengths and deficits address differences in perceptual functioning (C.4).
Mental disturbance including psychosis addresses types of hallucinatory experience (G.2.b) and hallucinations (G.2).
Emotional influences on cognition connect to determinants of attention (A.4). Cross-cultural variation relates to differences in perceptual functioning (C.4).
Drug effects on perception address factors affecting time perception (D.3). Psychoactive substances address hallucinations (G.2, G.2.b).

mappings.vsiAuthor: Steven W. Lockley, Russell G. Foster
mappings.rationaleAI: Sleep mechanisms affecting alertness connect to determinants of attention (A.4). Circadian regulation relates to perception of time (D).
Unconscious theory addresses determinants of attention (A.4). Dream interpretation connects to hallucinations (G.2).
Hallucinations and delusions address G.2. Brain imaging addresses neurological factors (G.2.a). Subjective experience addresses types of hallucination (G.2.b).
Social influence addresses determinants of attention (A.4). Social context effects on perception address C.3.b. Interpersonal perception addresses social space perception (E.4).
Hormonal effects on attentional states connect to determinants of attention (A.4). Endocrine-neural interactions address physiological mechanisms (A.5).
Treatment of perceptual disturbances in psychological disorders addresses hallucinations (G.2).
CBT's principle that cognition shapes experience connects to context effects on perception (C.3.b).
